titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 597

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=23 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:37:09.648562+00 | translation moa=24 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:37:09.668257+00

p.cl: W . Rex Scottorum . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem .

Sciant presentes et futuri me
Concessisse et hac carta mea Confirmasse Monachis de Goldincham Fiswich cum omni
bus ad illam iuste pertinentibus in terris et aquis . et dimidiam prendergest . et terram quam Swein
presbiter de fiswic habuit in Goldincham . et lummesdene . quas terras Swein presbiter de fiswic
in presencia Comitis henrici patris mei reddidit illis et quietas clammauit. Quare uolo ut predicti monachi prenominatas terras teneant ita libere et quiete plenarie et honorifice . sicut
carta Comitis henrici patris mei testatur et Confirmat.

Testibus . Eng’ episcopo de Glasgu .
Ricardo cumin . Dolph’ capellano . Roberto de Sancto Michele . Waltero de berkel’ . Hugone clerico meo .
Ricardo clerico .
William, king of the Scots, to all the worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

Let present future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to the monks of Coldingham, Fishwick with all things justly pertaining to it in lands and waters, and half of Prenderguest, and the land which Swain the priest of Fishwick had in Coldingham and Lumsdaine, those lands which Swain the priest of Fishwick gave back to them and quitclaimed in the presence of the Earl Henry my father. Wherefore I will that the monks may hold the forenamed lands as freely and quitly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the Earl Henry my father bears witness and affirms.
